preamble: The shift from a mid-back rounded nasalized vowel [õ] to a low nasalized vowel [ã] involves lowering and fronting, common in nasal vowel evolution where nasalization tends to lower high and mid vowels.

examples.note: Example of lowering and fronting of nasalized vowels in some French dialects or historical stages.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
